campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and the overall money situation. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, like adding bike lanes, bus routes, or pedestrian walkways.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and city finances
Treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving ways to get around the city

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could comment on a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced the ordinance and skipped the usual first-reading delay, with plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Details of specific ordinance topic not specified in agenda

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from multiple sessions held in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the full details of this project were cut off in the agenda provided.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple council sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contract (details incomplete)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026 to organize upcoming meetings. They reviewed and potentially added items to two upcoming agendas (April 21 and April 28), assigned time slots for specific discussion topics, and checked paperwork requirements. The council also reviewed official records from the previous week and discussed VEBA Advisory Committee matters.
keyTopics: April 21 final agenda finalization - adding items and scheduling time slots
April 28 draft agenda planning - organization of future discussions
Official public records review from April 2-9
VEBA Advisory Committee updates
Verification of proper notice and waiver requirements for agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what construction and improvement projects will get funding. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to highlight young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement budget and planned city projects
Teen Month proclamation recognizing young people in Mountain View
National Volunteer Week recognition for community volunteers

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review previous meeting notes and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update the city's residential tenant protections program to provide renters with stronger legal safeguards.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance amending tenant protection laws (Chapter 19.71)
Residential tenant protections program updates
Housing and human services policy review

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were invited to speak about any topics affecting the city (limited to 3 minutes each). The council also held elections to choose a new Chair and Vice Chair, and began discussing the budget for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address council on any city matter
Chair and Vice Chair elections for council leadership
Fiscal year budget presentation and discussion
State law limits council decisions on topics not officially on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for residents. If you disagree with a City decision (like a permit denial or zoning ruling), you have 90 days from the decision date to file a lawsuit, or you'll lose your right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the City over decisions
Legal challenges to permits and zoning rulings
Quasi-adjudicative decisions explained
California Civil Procedure Code requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plans for 2025-2026, review requests from individual council members for new projects or studies, and consider changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Changes to federal funding and community development plans
Council members' requests for new workplan items and projects
Amendment to rules governing how council vacancies are handled
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owners want to upgrade the building's historic status from Category 4 to Category 2, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of extra floor space as a bonus for renovating the building and improving its earthquake safety.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic status upgrade request
5,000 square feet bonus floor area for seismic improvements
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule
